alt_forms.note: Loanword-based equivalent. Wikipedia Japan (ターン (ゲーム)) cites ターンプレイヤー as a standard form.

definitions.gloss_en: the player whose turn it currently is; the active player
definitions.gloss_jp: その{時点|じてん}で{手|て}{番|ばん}を{持|も}つプレイヤー
definitions.explanation_en: Designates which player is currently empowered to take actions. Used in rulebook instructions to direct commands at the player whose turn is running.
definitions.implicit_notes: The compound foregrounds whose obligation it is to act, not merely whose right. Contrasts structurally with 非手番プレイヤー.
definitions.orthography_notes: Compound of native Japanese 手番 + katakana loanword プレイヤー. The mixed script is conventional in modern Japanese game rulebooks. No pure-kanji or pure-katakana equivalent is established.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Primarily board game and card game usage. Wikipedia Japan confirms ターンプレイヤー as a standard board game term; 手番プレイヤー is the preferred native-compound form.

usage_jp: {手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤーは～を{行|おこな}う (the active player performs ～)
usage_en: "active player" is the standard English equivalent in rulebook prose.
comparative_notes: ターンプレイヤー is equally acceptable and may be preferred in contexts using ターン rather than 手番 as the turn-word throughout.

examples.japanese: {手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤーは{移|い}{動|どう}{段|だん}{階|かい}を{開|かい}{始|し}する。
examples.english: The active player begins the moving phase.

cross_references.note: 非手番プレイヤー — the reactive / non-active player
手番 — the turn concept itself
